title: Checkpoints, Write-Ahead State, Crash Consistency, and Resume · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A delayed incident review reconstructs checkpoints, write-ahead state, crash consistency, and resume from revision, authorization, seed, URL, policy, response bytes, parsed identity, record provenance, frontier transition, Promise settlement, browser state, report row, failure, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for versioned crash-consistent crawl checkpoint after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-ca…
objectives: Define which policy, seed, frontier, attempt, digest, report, and output states must survive and which can be recomputed.
Write bounded versioned state to a temporary file, sync by declared durability, validate, rename atomically, and retain recovery copies.
Return claimed or fetching items to an explicit retry state using attempt and idempotency rules after a crash.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

stimulus.title: Resume Reconciliation case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review TypeScript team handles case 3896 during an origin-fairness and event-loop capacity rehearsal. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove state boundaries, atomic replacement, in-flight recovery, policy-version admission, and resume reconciliation; release evidence binds source, compiler, emitted JavaScript, lockfile, package, browser revision, container, configuration, and restored state; then reorder one frontier, p-limit, Promise, AbortSignal, stream, retry, checkpoint, or shutdown event and prove ownership. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Prove no lost terminal records, duplicate external effects, missing frontier items, or unexplained count changes across interruption.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A resumed process reaching the end proves correct recovery.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Prove no lost terminal records, duplicate external effects, missing frontier items, or unexplained count changes across interruption. Artifact under review: versioned crash-consistent crawl checkpoint.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Resume Reconciliation, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

For separate evidence from confidence, fetch one convenient page, omit owner authorization, source and emitted revision, URL and robots policy, runtime admission, stream and Promise ownership, Cheerio and browser differences, accessibility states, changed fixtures, recovery, and controlled Node transfer, then accept plausible objects as TypeScript crawler proof.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Record what required effort · Review Resume Reconciliation
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Resume Reconciliation: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review TypeScript team handles case 8753 during a multilingual archive markup migration. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove state boundaries, atomic replacement, in-flight recovery, policy-version admission, and resume reconciliation; URL, robots, body, parser, frontier, privacy, accessibility, and report budgets stay explicit; then change one owner permission, seed, URL, robots, sitemap, redirect, DNS, media, or encoding decision and reconcile provenance. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Prove no lost terminal records, duplicate external effects, missing frontier items, or unexplained count changes across interruption.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Record what required effort · Review Resume Reconciliation.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Record what required effort · Review Resume Reconciliation, keep Resume Reconciliation connected to executable TypeScript authorization, URL, runtime admission, Fetch, Promise, browser, accessibility, report, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Prove no lost terminal records, duplicate external effects, missing frontier items, or unexplained count changes across interruption.

hints: For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Record what required effort · Review Resume Reconciliation, name the owner, authorization, source and emitted revision, Node, TypeScript, URL, robots, unknown value, Fetch, Promise, AbortSignal, browser, accessibility, report, handle, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Resume Reconciliation precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Record what required effort · Review Resume Reconciliation, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A review TypeScript team handles case 8753 during a multilingual archive markup migration. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove state boundaries, atomic replacement, in-flight recovery, policy-version admission, and resume reconciliation; URL, robots, body, parser, frontier, privacy, accessibility, and report budgets stay explicit; then change one owner permission, seed, URL, robots, sitemap, redirect, DNS, media, or encoding decision and reconcile provenance. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Prove no lost terminal records, duplicate external effects, missing frontier items, or unexplained count changes across interruption.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Record what required effort · Review Resume Reconciliation, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A resumed process reaching the end proves correct recovery.
